mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower equity discount rate, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month. The effect is directional only when policy is actively easing; flat or rising rates do not carry the same positive return signal.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns are not higher after months with declining FEDFUNDS than after flat/rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign reverses and easing months produce negative average forward returns over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in RSAFS indicates accelerating nominal consumer demand and revenue growth, which supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when sales growth broadens beyond a one-month noise move.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered RSAFS up-moves do not produce positive average 20d SPX returns, or if the hit rate under triggered months falls below 55% for any full calendar-year sample after conditioning on positive sales growth.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises from already low levels, it signals labor-market deterioration before earnings revisions turn negative, causing S&P 500 returns to weaken over the following quarter. This mechanism relies on a weakening labor backdrop rather than the absolute unemployment level alone.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d SPX returns are not lower after UNRATE increases of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, or if the conditional forward return distribution is indistinguishable from baseline at p <= 0.05 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with rising PAYEMS identifies synchronized industrial output and employment growth, a broad expansion signal that supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The interaction matters because output growth without hiring, or hiring without output growth, is less informative.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of higher INDPRO and higher PAYEMS does not outperform single-series conditions on 20d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and construction activity, which precedes softer cyclical growth and lower S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect should appear after the market begins to price slower domestic demand, not on isolated monthly noise.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month declines in HOUST are followed by positive average 60d SPX returns, or if the signal fails to beat unconditional returns in triggered episodes across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays below the inflation trend, real policy rates compress and equity valuation multiples rise, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when policy has already tightened enough to offset inflation pressure.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3m annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: Reject if periods with inflation above the policy rate do not show higher 20d SPX returns than periods with inflation below the policy rate, or if the spread is negative for more than two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth accompanied by expanding retail sales indicates broad final demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal requires both aggregate output and household spending to be firm at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not yield better 60d SPX returns than either series alone, or if the joint signal underperforms unconditional returns in any full recession-to-expansion cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In late-expansion conditions, a low UNRATE combined with continued PAYEMS gains marks a durable labor market and supports near-term equity performance. The same labor readings lose power once unemployment starts rising, so the signal is specifically tied to a stable low-jobless regime.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.0% and PAYEMS is rising
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with stable labor market
failure_condition: Reject if low-UNRATE plus rising-PAYEMS episodes do not outperform baseline 5d SPX returns, or if the signal turns negative after conditioning on the same labor-state for more than one year.
